name: PDE5 Comparative Label Pharmacokinetic And Safety Boundary Extract
population_or_context: Regulatory labels for tadalafil, sildenafil, vardenafil, and avanafil ED/BPH-adjacent use; used to define class-transfer boundaries for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
sample_size: Not applicable; comparative extraction from four U.S. prescribing labels.
methods: Structured comparison of current or latest accessible DailyMed label sections covering dosing, contraindications, warnings, pharmacodynamics, pharmacokinetics, PDE selectivity, nitrate timing, and common adverse reactions.
population_profile: Labels are primarily ED/BPH male prescribing information and healthy-volunteer clinical pharmacology data; direct for drug-specific safety boundaries and indirect for off-label cognition, mood, exercise, cardiovascular, or longevity uses in mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Mixed PDE5 inhibitor label comparison: CIALIS tadalafil 2.5 to 20 mg including daily and as-needed use; VIAGRA sildenafil 25 to 100 mg as needed; vardenafil 2.5 to 20 mg as needed; STENDRA avanafil 50 to 200 mg as needed.
class_transfer_limits: Drug-label transfer is strongest for shared class contraindications such as nitrates/GC stimulators and weakest for timing, duration, daily exposure, food effects, PDE selectivity, QT warnings, and adverse-event patterns. Tadalafil-specific conclusions require tadalafil records because tadalafil has materially longer half-life and daily-use labeling.
indication_context: On-label ED/BPH regulatory safety context used as a framework for off-label evidence interpretation.
study_design: Comparative label synthesis, not a clinical trial or systematic review.
comparator_context: Cross-agent comparison of regulatory label statements rather than randomized head-to-head comparison.
safety_findings: All four ED PDE5 labels contraindicate organic nitrate use and GC-stimulator co-use and warn about hypotension with alpha-blockers/antihypertensives or alcohol; agent-specific labels differ in nitrate waiting intervals, pharmacokinetic half-life, CYP3A4 dose restrictions, vardenafil QT precautions, and PDE6/PDE11 selectivity language.
directness_to_target_population: High for medication-specific safety rules and agent-transfer boundaries; low to moderate for estimating absolute off-label risk in mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Labels are authoritative for approved use but are not designed to compare off-label cognition, mood, performance, cardiovascular prevention, or longevity outcomes across agents.
